item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Just for curiosity, what was the original sentence?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That’s an interesting point. I feel that would be interesting to keep, that he did have empathy issues. That said, I didn’t portray it well. You live and learn. I’m currently writing another character focused piece, and I’ll keep the advice I got for this one in mind, so I don’t repeat the same mistakes.

I can go edit some of the dialogue as well to try and make it less awkward.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The conversion with Casey was supposed to help inform the reader about what Rivas is like.

That's the point. Casey is not a character, but a exposition bot that exists to say out loud what your readers can figure out by themselves. Even Casey's speech pattern is odd, making unnecesary questions and leaving a feeling that, if he was an actual human being, he actually would have some empathy issues.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It seems just having a file page on the SCP would have worked just as well, I've had a few people tell me that. I'll keep that in mind.

Okay so I'm gonna admit, I'm gonna be a little stubborn with the characterization. Many people have told me that it falls a little flat, and I have been making notes so I can go back and make some edits. The conversion with Casey was supposed to help inform the reader about what Rivas is like.

Thanks for your feedback, it's much appreciated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think this has 2 very important flaws.

1: The main plot device (the meta nature of the article) is kinda wastes. You could've just made this in another way. And yeah, I get it is in order to fit several skips in a single article, but the effects falls flat.

2: The characterization is absolutely unbelieveable. All the characters need work, but Dr. Casey¿s conversation with Rivas was a huge block of exposition.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh, I saw it as believable because his attachment to the animals actually began as a symptom of his OCD: hoarding animals. As he grew distant from humans, he actually began to grow emotionally attached to them as well. I have OCD myself so I'm going to admit, I'm very stubborn about admitting I messed up in portraying any of the symptoms. I've tried to fix up Rivas' character, but in the end, I don't know what will do it. Thanks for your feed back.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Times like this I wish the voting system were more nuanced than kill it, save it, or leave it.

Dr. Rivas is compelling, but I think he could bear more story. His attachment to the anomalies he cares for isn't believable, rather than seeing it develop, we're handed it as an arbitrary fact. I would have no issue with this except the hook of the skip is that very thing.

I don't like it, as is, but I don't think it should die.

no-vote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article actually takes place in the past, but if you're refering to 4011, yeah lol.

Thanks! That means a lot! And yeah, big F. OCD is very hard to deal with without proper treatment, personal experience here haha. I'm undergoing good treatment now, so I use it to inspire me. Haha, sorry for the ramble. Thanks for the +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love it! Weird excuse for going into the future, and odd article overall, but it's really nice. F for doctor Rivas, OCD sucks for all of us :c

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad to finally have it up!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 'twas fun critiquing this. I am glad to see this on the main site now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know I was about to make this same comment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you. :) I'll try and fix up the clinical tone a bit. I've been having trouble with the dialouge, aha. Actively trying to fix it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Clinical tone and dialogue is a bit weak here in my view, but the idea is interesting overall. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for your feedback. :) And I was sorta knocking down the canon wall there, aha. But I get how that is bothersome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I took out the additional notes section, and added a bit to both interview sections in Rivas' personnel file. I asked some people if I should change it from list pages, but the majority thought it was fine. If there is anything I can do to fix up Rivas' character more, let me know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Unfortunately my concerns from the first version of this article remain. I don't think the framing device buys you anything, and the doctor isn't characterized nearly enough to have the desired impact.

To be honest, I'm not really sure what changed, other than the use of offsets rather than bare fragments and the addition of the offices theme. The text seemed essentially the same from the first time around?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really liked the article. Minor nitpick, though, I wouldn’t put any future SCPs in the article just so you don’t make it look weird when that SCP is just a sentient potted plant who makes knock knock jokes.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Yo, I've been working on this for a bit. Originally, the sentence was six words long, and I spent time trying to make filler documents when I eventually realised that I should just shorten the sentence.

Thanks to DrChandra, Oboebandgeek99, TheBlueHoue, AlanDaris, LittleFieryOne, Gee0765 WittleJohn, and Legotrekktor for the feedback, and those who helped me fix it up were Aismalard, and RadioactiveRADS. If you gave me feedback and I forgot to credit you, just bop me on the head in the comments.

Dr. Rivas means a lot to me as a character, as I have OCD myself, and it is the opposite of fun, so it was interesting to write a character for the wiki with it.
